summary: postinstall downloads and executes unpinned AppImage from GitHub /releases/latest with persistent autostart
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook chains into `bin/twokey.js --desktop --enable-autostart`, which queries `https://api.github.com/repos/meinzeug/twokey/releases/latest`, downloads the first `.AppImage` asset to `~/.local/share/twokey/bin/twokey-ai.AppImage`, chmods it 0755, spawns it detached, and installs a systemd user service at `~/.config/systemd/user/twokey.service` that re-launches the binary on every login. The fetch targets the mutable `/releases/latest` pointer rather than the version matching the installed npm package, and no hash or signature verification is performed on the downloaded bytes. When `npm install` is invoked as root with `SUDO_USER` set, `bin/postinstall.js` re-spawns the flow via `sudo -u <SUDO_USER> -H node bin/twokey.js --desktop --enable-autostart` so the unverified binary and persistence land in the desktop user's account. The publisher and purpose are plausibly aligned (the AppImage is the package's own desktop build hosted in the maintainer's GitHub repo), but the combination of unpinned mutable source + no integrity check + automatic execution at install time + silent systemd autostart means any future compromise of the upstream repo or release process would deliver arbitrary code to every installer, with persistence already wired up. This exceeds the bar for legitimate native-binary install (which requires version-pinned URL or hash verification).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
